description: Provider-agnostic multi-agent project management: skills + PTY overlay shell

tasks.title: Package scaffold
tasks.description: pyproject.toml with entry points, agentflow/__init__.py, agentflow/cli.py with full argparse structure (subcommands delegate to modules via lazy imports — NotImplementedError until modules built), and empty __init__.py for every subpackage. CLI entry point: agentflow → agentflow.cli:main.

unit: package is importable
all subpackages importable
agentflow --help prints usage

tasks.acceptance_criteria: pip install -e . succeeds and agentflow --help prints all subcommands

tasks.title: Config system
tasks.description: Pydantic v2 schema for all config fields (models, shell, testing, token_budget, file_limits, parallelism). Layered loader resolving env vars → .agentflow/config.yaml → ~/.agentflow/config.yaml → defaults.yaml. Expose load_config(cwd) → typed Config object. Ship defaults.yaml per architecture.md#config-schema.

unit: env var overrides project config
project config overrides user config
user config overrides defaults
invalid config raises ValidationError with field name
missing config files fall through to defaults cleanly
load_config returns typed Config with correct precedence

security_constraints: never log config values that may contain API keys
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all unit tests green; load_config returns typed Config with correct precedence

tasks.title: Telemetry — logger, token tracker, ledger
tasks.description: Structured JSON logger emitting JSONL to .agentflow/telemetry.jsonl per architecture.md#telemetry-schema. Token tracker: track_span(task_id, span, tokens_in, tokens_out) → appends to ledger; session_total(task_id); project_total(); shadow model accumulation; BudgetExceeded signal (not raise) at per_worker threshold; warning at 80%. Ledger r/w to .agentflow/ledger.json.

unit: emit_metric writes valid JSON line to telemetry.jsonl
trace_id propagates across nested calls
track_span appends record to ledger file
warning emitted at 80% of per_worker budget
BudgetExceeded returned at 100% not raised
shadow_total accumulates correctly across task restarts
telemetry must not contain prompt text — assert on output content

security_constraints: telemetry must not capture prompt content — token counts only
ledger must not contain prompt text or API keys
tasks.acceptance_criteria: BudgetExceeded signal returned at threshold; shadow ratio calculable; no prompt content in JSONL

tasks.title: Symbol indexer — index manager
tasks.description: Cache path resolution: ~/.agentflow/cache/<sha256-of-project-root>/index/<mirrored-path>.idx. Expose get_index(project_root, file_path) → parsed index dict (cache hit) or None (miss). Expose update_index(project_root, file_path, contents) → writes .idx file by dispatching to correct parser. Cache miss on get → regenerate from disk if source file exists. Index files are JSON.

unit: get_index returns None on cache miss
update_index writes .idx to correct cache path
cache path is deterministic given same project root
get_index returns parsed dict on cache hit
index file absent but source exists triggers regeneration

tasks.acceptance_criteria: index path deterministic; cache miss triggers regen; index files never in project tree

tasks.title: Symbol indexer — parsers
tasks.description: Four parsers returning {symbol_name: {type, signature, start_line, end_line, summary}} dicts. python_parser: ast.parse → functions and classes with signatures and docstring first line. markdown_parser: regex H2/H3 headers → section name + line range. json_parser: json.loads → top-level keys + line numbers (skip files ≤ 30 lines). yaml_parser: regex top-level and second-level keys (skip files ≤ 30 lines). All stdlib, no external deps.

unit: python_parser extracts function name, signature, and line range
python_parser extracts class name and methods
markdown_parser extracts H2 and H3 headers with line ranges
json_parser returns empty dict for files ≤ 30 lines
yaml_parser extracts top-level and second-level keys
unknown extension returns empty dict without error

tasks.acceptance_criteria: all parsers return correct symbol dicts; no external deps; files ≤ 30 lines skipped

tasks.title: PTY wrapper
tasks.description: Spawn an AI CLI command (e.g. 'claude', 'gemini') inside a PTY using the pty module. Transparently relay I/O between user terminal and child process. Expose read_output(timeout) → bytes, write_input(text) → None. Detect child process exit and surface ExitCode. All I/O callbacks registered at construction time for interception. stdlib only.

unit: write_input sends bytes to child stdin
read_output returns bytes from child stdout
child exit triggers exit callback with code
I/O callbacks receive all bytes passing through
integration: spawn echo process in PTY, verify output relayed correctly

tasks.acceptance_criteria: integration test passes with real PTY; I/O transparent to child process

tasks.title: Local tokenizer
tasks.description: Per-provider token counting using tiktoken. count_tokens(text, provider) → int. Supported providers: 'claude', 'gemini' (both use cl100k_base approximation). Maintain a running total via accumulate(text, provider) → int (cumulative). reset() clears the accumulator. No API calls. Fallback to whitespace split if tiktoken unavailable (degrade gracefully, log warning).

unit: count_tokens returns positive int for non-empty text
accumulate adds to running total correctly
reset sets total to zero
both providers return same tokenizer (cl100k_base)
unknown provider raises ValueError

tasks.acceptance_criteria: token counts within 5% of Anthropic API reported counts on sample prompts; no API calls made

tasks.title: PTY session manager and countdown
tasks.description: Session manager: wraps PTYWrapper and Tokenizer. Watches I/O for session_type detection (first /oracle or /orchestrate in stdin). Accumulates tokens. On threshold breach: calls trigger_handoff() which injects /handoff, scans stdout for HANDOFF_COMPLETE, then passes to countdown. Suppresses auto-restart if /handoff came from user (not injected). countdown.py: prints '...restarting in Ns (Ctrl+C to cancel)', handles SIGINT, calls restart callback on expiry.

unit: session_type set to oracle on /oracle in stdin
session_type set to orchestrator on /orchestrate in stdin
threshold breach triggers handoff injection
user-initiated /handoff suppresses auto-restart
HANDOFF_COMPLETE pattern parsed correctly from stdout
countdown fires restart callback after delay
SIGINT during countdown cancels restart

tasks.acceptance_criteria: threshold breach triggers full flow: inject /handoff → detect HANDOFF_COMPLETE → countdown → restart skill

tasks.title: Git tools
tasks.description: Worktree lifecycle: create_worktree(base_path, task_id, branch_name) → Path, delete_worktree(path), commit_files(worktree_path, message, files). All operations idempotent. Typed exceptions: WorktreeExistsError, DirtyStateError, MissingRepoError. No shell=True — subprocess list args only. Branch names validated against allowlist pattern before use.

unit: create_worktree creates branch and directory
create_worktree is idempotent if worktree already exists
delete_worktree removes directory and prunes ref
commit_files stages only listed files
invalid branch name raises ValueError before subprocess call
integration: full worktree create → commit → delete cycle on real temp repo

security_constraints: no shell=True in any subprocess call
validate branch names against ^[a-zA-Z0-9/_-]+$ before use
tasks.acceptance_criteria: integration test passes on real git repo; no shell=True in source

tasks.title: GitHub tools
tasks.description: PR creation, inline comment posting, status check reading via GitHub REST API. create_pr(repo, branch, title, body) → pr_number. post_review_comments(repo, pr_number, comments: list[ReviewComment]) — batched in one call. get_check_status(repo, pr_number) → CheckResult. Auth via GITHUB_TOKEN env var only. httpx, not requests. Retry 3× on rate limit with exponential backoff.

unit: create_pr posts to correct endpoint with correct payload
post_review_comments batches comments in one API call
missing GITHUB_TOKEN raises AuthError not KeyError
4xx responses raise GitHubAPIError with status code
rate limit response triggers retry with backoff
repo name format validated before URL construction

security_constraints: GITHUB_TOKEN read from env only — never from config files
validate repo name format before constructing URL
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all unit tests green with mocked httpx; AuthError on missing token; no token in logs

tasks.title: Test runner and file validator
tasks.description: test_runner: run_tests(worktree_path, config) → TestResult(passed, failed, coverage_pct, coverage_ok, status). Detect framework from config (pytest default). Parse coverage from pytest-cov output. Timeout returns TestResult(status='timeout'). file_validator: validate_file(path) → ValidationResult(ok, line_count, limit, message). Enforces file_limits from config. Paths validated to be within project root.

unit: run_tests returns TestResult with correct pass/fail counts
coverage below threshold sets coverage_ok=False
file_validator flags files over ceiling with path and line count
file_validator returns clean for files under soft target
run_tests timeout returns status=timeout
subprocess path validated within project root
integration: run_tests against minimal pytest project in temp dir

security_constraints: subprocess runs in worktree_path only; validate path is within project root before execution
tasks.acceptance_criteria: integration test passes; coverage_ok reflects threshold comparison; path escape rejected

tasks.title: write_file tool with index hook
tasks.description: write_file(project_root, path, contents) → WriteResult. Validates path is within owns list (SandboxViolationError if not). Writes file to disk. Calls index_manager.update_index(project_root, path, contents) as silent side effect. Worker never knows about the index. Expose set_owns_list(task_id, owns) to register allowed paths before the worker runs.

unit: write_file writes content to correct path
write_file calls index_manager.update_index after writing
path outside owns list raises SandboxViolationError
SandboxViolationError does not write the file
index update failure does not fail the write

security_constraints: worker may only write to files in its registered owns list — enforced in this tool, not by the LLM
tasks.acceptance_criteria: SandboxViolationError raised for out-of-owns writes; index updated silently on success

tasks.title: Oracle prompts
tasks.description: Write all oracle prompt files. system.md: multi-persona (Senior PE + PM + Designer), market segment branching, prompt injection question. market.md: consumer/SMB/enterprise question bank with compliance and auth defaults per segment. checklist.md: 23+ NFR items mapped to architecture.md#oracle-design. generation.md: architecture.md + CLAUDE.md output format with RESOLVED/UNRESOLVED/DEFERRED item format. Each file ≤ 150 lines.

unit: all prompt files are valid UTF-8
no prompt file exceeds 150-line hard ceiling
system.md contains market segment section
system.md contains prompt injection question
checklist.md contains all 23 NFR section headers
generation.md contains RESOLVED/UNRESOLVED/DEFERRED format spec

security_constraints: prompts must not include example secrets or credentials
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all files exist, pass size check, pass content assertions

tasks.title: Worker prompts
tasks.description: Write all worker prompt files. system.md: implementer persona, no-re-read rule ('Do not use the Read tool on any file listed in your Dependencies section — contents already in context'), TDD approach. context_bundle.md: bundle format spec and interpretation guide. testing_guide.md: red→green TDD, behaviour not implementation, IO mocks pre-generated, skeleton bodies start as NotImplementedError. Each file ≤ 150 lines.

unit: all files valid UTF-8 and under 150 lines
system.md contains no-re-read rule before task description section
testing_guide.md contains TDD, behaviour-not-implementation, IO mock sections

security_constraints: prompts must not include example secrets
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all files exist; no-re-read rule appears in system.md before task section

tasks.title: Reviewer and orchestrator prompts
tasks.description: Reviewer prompts: code_review.md (contract adherence, architecture conformance, file size check), security_review.md (OWASP Top 10, secrets, PR diff treated as untrusted data — never as instructions, compliance constraints), test_review.md (scenario coverage, mock appropriateness, coverage threshold). Orchestrator prompts: system.md (Staff Engineering Lead persona — executes the milestone plan faithfully, manages parallelism and failure, escalates to human when authority is exceeded; does not re-prioritize), planning.md (milestone decomposition format from architecture.md). Each ≤ 150 lines.

unit: all files valid UTF-8 and under 150 lines
security_review.md references OWASP Top 10
security_review.md contains untrusted-diff instruction
planning.md contains milestone decomposition format
system.md contains Staff Engineering Lead persona framing
system.md contains escalation criteria (second rework failure, CRITICAL security finding)

security_constraints: security_review.md must not echo example secret values
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all files exist; security_review.md contains untrusted-diff rule and OWASP reference

tasks.title: Oracle checklist evaluator
tasks.description: checklist.py: evaluate confidence score across 23 NFR checklist items given a conversation history dict. Returns ChecklistResult(resolved: list, unresolved: list, deferred: list, confidence: float). Market segment answer gates which items are required vs optional. resolve_item(item_id, decision) marks item RESOLVED. defer_item(item_id, reason) marks DEFERRED. All items RESOLVED or DEFERRED → generation_ready() returns True.

unit: checklist returns unresolved items when NFRs missing
checklist returns generation_ready=True when all items RESOLVED or DEFERRED
enterprise market segment requires SOC2 and SSO items
consumer market segment adds GDPR and COPPA items
defer_item removes item from unresolved, adds to deferred

tasks.acceptance_criteria: generation_ready() accurate for all market segments; deferred items not blocking

tasks.title: Oracle artifact generator
tasks.description: artifact_generator.py: given resolved ChecklistResult and conversation summary, write architecture.md (with all design items as RESOLVED/DEFERRED entries per format spec), CLAUDE.md, and tasks.json. Validate tasks.json: no two tasks share owned files; estimated_lines ≤ 250 per file. Input sanitisation: scan user-provided project description for instruction-override patterns before embedding in artifacts. Orchestrator reads tasks.json and may extend it via lazy milestone decomposition.

unit: artifact_generator writes architecture.md with RESOLVED/DEFERRED items
artifact_generator writes CLAUDE.md
artifact_generator writes valid tasks.json with all tasks pending
instruction-override pattern in description raises SanitisationError
architecture.md contains all sections from generation.md format spec

security_constraints: scan input for prompt injection patterns before embedding in output artifacts
tasks.acceptance_criteria: architecture.md, CLAUDE.md, and tasks.json written; ownership conflicts rejected; injection patterns rejected

tasks.title: Worker context builder
tasks.description: context_builder.py: assemble minimal context bundle for a task and write to .agentflow/context/<task-id>.md. Include: task brief, owned file list, read-only file contents (do not re-read signal), contract stub contents, relevant architecture.md section by anchor, security constraints, test scenarios, config snapshot. Use index_manager.get_index to include symbol lookups for referenced files. Emit bundle token count as metric. Warn if bundle > 50k tokens.

unit: bundle contains task description and acceptance criteria
bundle contains owned file list
bundle excludes files not in owns or reads
bundle includes only the anchored section of architecture.md
token count metric emitted after build
warning emitted when bundle exceeds 50k tokens
bundle must not include API keys from env

security_constraints: bundle must not include GITHUB_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, or GEMINI_API_KEY
tasks.acceptance_criteria: bundle contains exactly specified sections; token metric emitted; no secrets in bundle

tasks.title: Headless worker agent runner
tasks.description: agent_runner.py: run a headless API agent for a single task. Read .agentflow/context/<task-id>.md as opening user message. Agent loop: call provider API → parse tool calls (read_file, write_file via write_file_tool, run_tests) → continue until tests green and coverage met → call create_pr. Track token spans per API call via token_tracker. On BudgetExceeded signal: compress progress summary and restart with fresh session (max 2 restarts). On 3rd: return WorkerResult(status='escalate').

unit: run returns WorkerResult with pr_number on success
run returns WorkerResult(status=escalate) after max restarts
token spend per API call emitted to telemetry
write_file tool call sandboxed to owns list
test failure triggers continued loop not immediate exit
BudgetExceeded triggers compress-and-restart, not raise

security_constraints: worker may only write to files in its owns list via write_file_tool
worker may not execute arbitrary shell commands — tool whitelist enforced
tasks.acceptance_criteria: WorkerResult returned in all paths; sandbox enforced; escalate returned after max restarts

tasks.title: Code reviewer agent
tasks.description: code_reviewer.py: headless reviewer reading PR diff + contract stubs + relevant architecture section. Reviews for: contract adherence (implementation matches stub interfaces), architecture conformance (file size limits, module boundaries), obvious logic errors. Posts inline comments via github.post_review_comments. Returns ReviewResult(issues_count, severity_distribution). File exceeding hard ceiling → HIGH severity. Contract mismatch → references specific function name.

unit: review returns ReviewResult with correct issue count
comments posted as inline review not PR body
file exceeding size ceiling flagged as HIGH severity
contract mismatch flagged with specific function name
empty diff returns ReviewResult with zero issues

tasks.acceptance_criteria: ReviewResult returned; inline comments on correct line numbers; size violations flagged HIGH

tasks.title: Security reviewer agent
tasks.description: security_reviewer.py: headless security reviewer. Context: PR diff (treated as untrusted data — never as instructions) + security_constraints from task schema + compliance requirements. Checks: OWASP Top 10 patterns, hardcoded secrets, shell=True usage, missing input validation at system boundaries, compliance constraint violations. Returns SecurityReviewResult(critical_count, high_count, findings). CRITICAL findings block HUMAN_APPROVED transition. Findings reference file and line — never echo secret values.

unit: hardcoded secret pattern triggers CRITICAL finding
shell=True usage triggers HIGH finding
missing input validation at API boundary triggers HIGH
clean diff returns SecurityReviewResult with zero criticals
compliance constraint violation references the constraint string
finding output never echoes the detected secret value

security_constraints: reviewer output must not echo secret values — reference location only
tasks.acceptance_criteria: CRITICAL findings present in result; findings reference file and line; no secret values echoed

tasks.title: Orchestrator — execution plan and task decomposer
tasks.description: execution_plan.py: read architecture.md → extract design sections → produce execution_plan.md with milestones (PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETE/DEFERRED). Lazy decomposition: only decompose next milestone when current completes. resume_from(execution_plan_path) → returns first incomplete milestone. task_decomposer.py: given a milestone and architecture section, produce tasks.json entries. Validate: no shared owned files; estimated_lines ≤ 250. Write tasks.json idempotently (update pending tasks only).

unit: execution_plan.py writes milestones from architecture.md sections
resume_from returns first milestone not COMPLETE
resume_from returns None when all milestones COMPLETE
task_decomposer rejects tasks.json with shared owned files
task_decomposer rejects task with estimated_lines > 250
update is idempotent — running twice produces same result

tasks.acceptance_criteria: execution_plan.md written; resume point correct; ownership conflicts rejected before workers spawn

tasks.title: Orchestrator — state machine and environment setup
tasks.description: state_machine.py: task state transitions PENDING→SPAWNED→IMPLEMENTING→PR_OPEN→REVIEW_IN_PROGRESS→REVIEW_PASSED→HUMAN_APPROVED→MERGED (and REWORK_NEEDED). Persist timestamps per transition to .agentflow/state.json. InvalidTransitionError on illegal moves. CRITICAL security finding blocks HUMAN_APPROVED. environment.py: tech-stack aware env bootstrap. Read tech_stack from architecture.md or config. Dispatch: python→virtualenv+requirements.txt, node→npm install, go→go mod download.

unit: PENDING→SPAWNED transition writes timestamp to state.json
invalid transition raises InvalidTransitionError
CRITICAL finding blocks HUMAN_APPROVED transition
load_state returns empty state when file absent
environment.py dispatches to virtualenv for python tech stack
environment.py dispatches to npm for node tech stack

security_constraints: state.json must not contain prompt content or API keys
tasks.acceptance_criteria: all transitions correct; CRITICAL blocks HUMAN_APPROVED; state.json clean of secrets

tasks.title: Orchestrator — project manager and merge sequencer
tasks.description: project_manager.py: PM loop reading tasks.json + state.json. Spawns workers for READY tasks up to parallelism limit. Handles WorkerResult: success→PR_OPEN transition + trigger reviewer pipeline; escalate→human notification + halt task. Triggers code_reviewer and security_reviewer after PR_OPEN. Transitions state on reviewer completion. merge_sequencer.py: after HUMAN_APPROVED, merges worktree branches into main in topological DAG order, deletes worktrees, updates milestone status in execution_plan.md.

unit: ready tasks spawned up to parallelism limit
task with unmet dependency not spawned
WorkerResult escalate triggers notification and halts task
CRITICAL security finding blocks HUMAN_APPROVED transition
merge sequencer processes tasks in topological order
failed merge rolls back without corrupting state.json

tasks.acceptance_criteria: full lifecycle runs PENDING→MERGED; state.json accurate throughout; CRITICAL blocks merge

tasks.title: Handoff skill — provider files
tasks.description: Write handoff skill files for Claude and Gemini. Both flush current session state to the living document (architecture.md for oracle sessions, execution_plan.md for orchestrator sessions) and print HANDOFF_COMPLETE: <path> to stdout for the PTY shell to detect. Skills also emit HANDOFF RECOMMENDED: <reason> proactively at natural stopping points during normal skill operation — oracle emits after each checklist resolution batch; orchestrator emits after each task reaches PR_OPEN or MERGED state. This is the pre-PTY manual signal: user reads it and triggers /handoff themselves. PTY will later detect and act on this signal automatically. Claude: agentflow/skills/providers/claude/handoff.md — provider-agnostic, HANDOFF_COMPLETE signal, .agentflow/ paths. Gemini: agentflow/skills/providers/gemini/handoff/SKILL.md + run_handoff.sh — structured output and HANDOFF_COMPLETE signal.

unit: claude/handoff.md contains HANDOFF_COMPLETE output instruction
claude/handoff.md uses .agentflow/ paths not .claude/memory/
gemini SKILL.md contains HANDOFF_COMPLETE output instruction
run_handoff.sh is executable and contains HANDOFF_COMPLETE echo
oracle skill emits HANDOFF RECOMMENDED after checklist batch resolution
orchestrator skill emits HANDOFF RECOMMENDED after task reaches PR_OPEN or MERGED
HANDOFF RECOMMENDED message includes reason string

tasks.acceptance_criteria: both skill files print HANDOFF_COMPLETE: <path>; both emit HANDOFF RECOMMENDED: <reason> at natural stopping points; no OCI-specific content; HANDOFF RECOMMENDED fires without /handoff being invoked

tasks.title: Oracle skill — provider files
tasks.description: Write oracle skill files for Claude and Gemini. Both implement the multi-persona design sparring flow, read architecture.md on startup to check for UNRESOLVED items, resume or start fresh accordingly, and call artifact_generator on completion. Claude: agentflow/skills/providers/claude/oracle.md. Gemini: agentflow/skills/providers/gemini/oracle/SKILL.md + scripts/run_oracle.sh. Skills must not hardcode project-specific content — they are generic across all projects.

unit: claude/oracle.md contains architecture.md UNRESOLVED check on startup
claude/oracle.md contains multi-persona persona declaration
claude/oracle.md does not reference any specific project
gemini SKILL.md contains equivalent startup check

tasks.acceptance_criteria: both skill files generic (no project-specific content); UNRESOLVED check on startup present

tasks.title: Orchestrator skill — provider files
tasks.description: Write orchestrator skill files for Claude and Gemini. Both read architecture.md (oracle must be complete — all RESOLVED or DEFERRED) then read execution_plan.md to resume or initialise. Decompose next incomplete milestone lazily. Spawn workers, manage reviews, gate on HUMAN_APPROVED, trigger merge sequencer. Claude: agentflow/skills/providers/claude/orchestrate.md. Gemini: agentflow/skills/providers/gemini/orchestrate/SKILL.md.

unit: claude/orchestrate.md contains oracle-complete gate check
claude/orchestrate.md contains HUMAN_APPROVED gate instruction
claude/orchestrate.md contains lazy milestone decomposition instruction
gemini SKILL.md contains equivalent checks

tasks.acceptance_criteria: both skill files contain oracle-complete check, HUMAN_APPROVED gate, lazy decomposition

tasks.title: Brownfield scanner
tasks.description: brownfield_scanner.py: on first load of an existing project, walk the project tree and index all files meeting the minimum line threshold (30 lines). Dispatch to correct parser by extension. Write index files to ~/.agentflow/cache/<hash>/index/. Skip files already in cache (check mtime). Report: files indexed, files skipped, total time. No refactoring — index only. Expose scan(project_root, config) → ScanResult(indexed, skipped, duration_ms).

unit: scan indexes all eligible files in project tree
scan skips files under 30-line threshold
scan skips files already in cache with current mtime
scan returns correct indexed and skipped counts
scan does not modify any source files
integration: scan against real temp project with mixed file types and sizes

tasks.acceptance_criteria: no source files modified; cache populated; mtime-based skip works correctly
